findings.finding: The candidate states that completion requires 10/10 and every rubric criterion, with recovery and retry after any miss. Each rendered open-response form instead exposes a textarea and the same model reasoning after submission. It does not determine or announce a score, identify missed rubric criteria, or programmatically link a learner to the matching recovery route. Keyboard and screen-reader users can operate the controls, but they cannot operate the declared criterion-level completion and feedback path.
findings.resolution: Provide an accessible, non-timed scoring and feedback route that visibly and programmatically exposes criterion status, score, and the relevant recovery/retry action for every item; or revise the completion language to describe an accurate self-comparison route without asserting an unimplemented 10/10 score. Re-test keyboard and screen-reader feedback after the change.

findings.finding: The v0.4.1 provenance update is internally coherent: six distinct author or revision runs are disclosed with system, provider, model, version, run IDs, and SHA-256 digests. The curriculum adaptation has an explicit CC BY 4.0 basis, immutable source link, modification notice, and dated checked-no-agent-restriction-found record. No third-party assets are declared or present.

Scene order, heading and list semantics, table column headers, keyboard focus return, narrow reflow, reduced-motion behavior, print continuity, text alternatives for the fictional table, untimed retry routes, and non-physical response alternatives were otherwise preserved. No raw HTML, executable content, remote embed, unsafe path, secret, personal health data, or sensory-only core information was found.

limitations: This is accessibility, rights, provenance, privacy, and content-security evidence checking, not legal advice; jurisdiction-specific conclusions remain subject to qualified counsel where needed.
No representative screen-reader application, physical touch device, or learner usability study was available; keyboard and programmatic semantics were inspected in headless Chromium.
A 390 CSS-pixel viewport was used as the repository's 200% reflow proxy; native browser zoom was not separately exercised.
Print media was exercised, but the unavailable non-web PDF, EPUB, DOCX, and JATS adapters could not be tested.
Author and reviewer provenance is disclosed and internally checked but self-attested rather than cryptographically verified.
One accessibility-and-rights review is not quorum, adjudication, or merge readiness.
